mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains at or above a cyclical peak level for multiple consecutive months (5.25% as of Nov-2006), the cumulative drag of restrictive real rates on corporate financing and consumer credit compresses forward equity returns over the next 20 trading days relative to periods of falling rates.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following high-rate plateaus are not significantly lower than the sample mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST exceeding 15% from its cyclical peak signals a housing-led demand contraction that precedes broader economic slowdown and equity market weakness over a quarterly horizon, as residential investment collapse historically leads GDP deceleration by 2-4 quarters.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 15% from trailing 12-month peak
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1491 units revised, down sharply from 2005 peaks near 2000+
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns following HOUST breach of the -15% threshold are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of decelerating PAYEMS month-over-month growth and a rising UNRATE signals an inflecting labor market that undermines consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative SPX returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone carries the same signal strength.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth decelerates while UNRATE rises simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% and PAYEMS 137,060k as of Nov-2006
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE rise instances produce positive 20d forward returns more than 55% of the time, or the interaction term's marginal Rank IC contribution is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate output, which is priced into equities with a short lag, producing positive SPX returns over the following 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 99.1627 as of Nov-2006
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 5d forward SPX return remains below 0.02 across any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Once the Federal Reserve begins cutting FEDFUNDS after a prolonged tightening plateau, the anticipation of lower discount rates and looser financial conditions drives equity markets higher over a quarterly horizon; the effect is specific to the transition into an easing cycle and is not present during a hiking or flat-rate regime.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines for 2+ consecutive FOMC meetings after a plateau of at least 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS unchanged at 5.25% since June 2006, plateau ongoing
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns following the first two rate cuts after a plateau are negative in more than 45% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) that outpaces concurrent CPIAUCSL inflation indicates rising real consumer purchasing power, a joint condition that signals durable demand strength and supports positive SPX returns over the next 20 trading days; RSAFS growth alone confounded by inflation does not carry the same signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth exceeds CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation by more than 1pp
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised 352,116M and CPIAUCSL revised 202.0 as of Nov-2006
failure_condition: Instances where real retail sales growth (RSAFS minus CPIAUCSL inflation) exceeds threshold produce negative 20d forward SPX returns more than 45% of the time, or the interaction Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window historically marks the early stage of a labor market downturn that precedes broader earnings deterioration, producing negative SPX returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE flat at 4.5% as of Nov-2006, near cycle lows
failure_condition: Following UNRATE threshold breaches, SPX 60d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of month-over-month decline in INDPRO signal an emerging industrial contraction that is not yet reflected in equity prices, leading to negative SPX returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as markets reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 112.3227 vs revised 99.1627 as of Nov-2006, indicating revision volatility
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns following two consecutive INDPRO monthly declines are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
